narrative: A well-shaped, ~113-line Simple Workflow with a clean customization surface, correct language-rule discipline, and the right institutional shape for a single-artifact D-06 generator. The biggest gap is that the Decision-Log Workspace pattern is half-built — resume/update on re-activation, headless interaction defaults, and sharded-PRD fallback are all incomplete — and Stage 1 is form-shaped where it should be facilitative; secondary wins live in collapsing On-Activation boilerplate and extracting three deterministic validators into a `scripts/` directory. POST-ANALYSIS UPDATE: three follow-on fixes applied — D-06 template renamed to English (Rule 1), template content rewritten as English i18n source (Rule 2), and SKILL.md language rules tightened to make template→output translation explicit (Rule 3). Five new opportunities added from BMad-base + HBC-sibling pattern review (B1–B3, B5, B7); two refinements (B6, B8). See quality-report.md Addendum for citations and updated priority order.

broken.title: Sharded PRD fallback reads only `index.md` and misses the substance
broken.detail: Stage 1 says 'search whole docs first, fall back to sharded `*/index.md`'. Sharded PRDs put actors and FRs in `prd/epics/*.md`, not in `index.md` — the fallback rule reads the table of contents and runs discovery on near-empty input. In guided mode the user gets a wasted elicitation loop; in headless mode the agent hallucinates.
broken.action: Change the rule to 'for sharded PRDs, read `index.md` to enumerate shard paths, then read every linked shard' (or shorter: 'read the full shard set, not just the index').

broken.title: .decision-log.md sits at skill root instead of references/
broken.detail: Path-standards lint flags it as a structure violation: progressive-disclosure content must live under `references/`, only `SKILL.md` belongs at root. The decision log is build-time scaffolding (LLM ideation memory for the skill author), not a runtime artifact the skill loads — but at root it confuses the standards scan and any auto-tooling that walks skill directories.
broken.action: Move to `references/.decision-log.md` (or delete if the build is final and the log was working memory only).

opportunities.name: Decision-Log Workspace pattern is half-built — resume, update, and compaction-survival are missing
opportunities.description: Workspace creation and decision-log append-on-existing are wired correctly, but the entire reason the pattern exists — resume on re-activation, Update mode for revisable artifacts, and flushing discovered state to disk so compaction doesn't wipe it — is not implemented. The downstream chain (architecture / UX / stories) will trigger D-06 re-runs every time the PRD changes; today each re-run is a fresh draft that forgets the prior session's reasoning, and the dated `output_folder_name` default actively fights resume by creating a new workspace on day 2.
opportunities.impact: Eliminates the single biggest user-facing gap: makes re-runs respect prior intent, lets users target one flow without rebuilding everything, and survives mid-session compaction in migration mode where discovery runs twice.
opportunities.action: On Stage 1 entry, after binding `{doc_workspace}`, check whether the primary exists; if so, read `.decision-log.md`, surface a one-line summary plus `updated` timestamp, and offer Create-fresh / Update-flows / Validate intents. After Stage 2 confirmation, flush the actor list and flow inventory to the decision log (or as draft sections of the primary) so Stage 3 renders from disk. On Update mode, read the latest version from the existing revision-history table and append a new row rather than overwriting. Document that resume looks across same-`{project_name}` folders (or recommend dropping `{date}` from the default folder name when re-runs are expected).

findings.title: Resume protocol absent for revisable artifact
findings.detail: Workspace creation is correct, but on re-activation the skill does not surface the prior primary, summarize the previous session, or offer an Update / Validate intent. Treats every run as a fresh create.

findings.title: Stage 2 holds discovered state only in the conversation
findings.detail: Stage 2 enumerates actors / triggers / steps / decisions / outcomes but doesn't flush them to the decision log. If compaction drops the conversation mid-discovery (especially migration mode where it runs twice), the AS-IS work is unrecoverable.

findings.title: Dated folder name fights resume by design
findings.detail: `output_folder_name = "D-06-{project_name}-{date}"` — re-running the next day creates a new workspace and orphans the previous decision log.

findings.title: Revision history hardcoded to version 1.0 — no update-mode bump
findings.detail: Stage 3 hardcodes initial-entry/version 1.0. Once resume is added, the revision-history append needs to read the latest version and increment, not overwrite.

opportunities.name: Headless mode is declared but the interaction contract is undefined
opportunities.description: `--headless` is named in the description, the JSON return shape is specified, and Stage 1 instructs to 'log every assumption' — but every interactive decision point in Stages 1, 2, and 4 lacks an explicit headless-default line. Different runs against the same PRD will silently choose different modes, scopes, and auto-fixes. There is also no documented input contract — automators can't override the inferred defaults — and the 'no PRD found in headless mode' branch is a self-contradicting elicitation fallback.
opportunities.impact: Makes headless invocation deterministic, auditable, and contract-driven so the downstream automator chain (the named consumers `bmad-create-architecture` / `-ux-design` / `-epics-and-stories`) gets reproducible D-06 output.
opportunities.action: Add a small headless-defaults table next to each Stage-1/2/4 decision point naming the heuristic that resolves it (e.g. PRD-contains-AS-IS-section -> migration). Declare the headless input contract: `--prd`, `--mode`, `--scope`, `--diagram-type`, `--no-prd-ok`. When PRD is absent and `--headless` is set, return `status: blocked` with a `reason` instead of falling into elicitation. Also rename or document the `primary` JSON field (non-standard vs canonical `report`/`artifact`).

findings.title: Headless interaction defaults undefined
findings.detail: Stage 1's four questions (sources, mode, scope, diagram type), Stage 2's actor confirmation, and Stage 4's collaborative-fix loop all have no headless-default lines.

findings.title: No documented headless input contract
findings.detail: Return shape is specified but input flags are not. Automators reading SKILL.md cannot tell what they're allowed to pass.

findings.title: Headless JSON contract uses non-standard field name `primary`
findings.detail: Canonical headless contracts use `report`/`artifact`/`document`. `primary` is skill-specific and undocumented — downstream callers won't know to look for it.

opportunities.name: Three deterministic validation operations live in the prompt — no scripts/ directory exists
opportunities.description: Stage 1 source-doc discovery (glob walk of `*prd*.md`, `*ux*.md`, `D-04*.md`, etc.) and Stage 4's three validation checks (Mermaid syntax parse, actor coverage, FR-to-flow traceability) are deterministic file-system and parsing work that the LLM currently re-does on every run. They burn ~2400-5200 tokens per invocation with no quality benefit and the Mermaid/actor-coverage checks have a real catch-rate gap the LLM eyeballing misses.
opportunities.impact: Recovers ~2400-5200 tokens per run and raises catch-rate on orphan Mermaid participants and uncovered FRs. Also closes the 'auto-fix in headless mode' quality risk (Stage 4's auto-fix promise has no real parse step behind it).
opportunities.action: Add a `scripts/` directory with three small Python scripts: (1) `discover-planning-artifacts.py` — the pre-pass that walks `{planning_artifacts}` and emits compact JSON of PRD/UX/research candidates; (2) `validate-mermaid.py` — extracts Mermaid blocks, parses participant/message declarations, accepts an `--expected-actors` arg, returns findings JSON (folds S2 and S4 together); (3) `check-fr-coverage.py` — extracts FR identifiers from PRD and D-06 and returns covered/uncovered/phantom sets. Wire them in as pre-pass (S1 before Stage 2) and post-pass (S2-S4 in Stage 4). The LLM keeps the judgment calls — actor identification, AS-IS/TO-BE delta, layout choice.

findings.title: PRD/UX/research discovery scan done in-prompt
findings.detail: LLM glob-walks `{planning_artifacts}` for `*prd*.md`, `*ux*.md`, `D-04*.md`, `D-05*.md`, `research/*.md`. ~600-1300 tokens/run of pure file-system work.

findings.title: Mermaid syntax validation done by reading
findings.detail: 'Each code block parses; no orphan messages or undeclared participants' is parsing + set membership. ~600-1000 tokens/run. In headless mode the auto-fix promise has no real parse step behind it.

findings.title: FR-to-flow traceability done by re-scanning the PRD
findings.detail: LLM extracts FR-* ids from PRD and cross-checks every Mermaid block. ~1000-2500 tokens/run on PRD-backed flows. Identifier-presence pre-pass is deterministic; only semantic coverage of uncovered FRs needs judgment.

findings.title: Actor-coverage cross-check done by re-reading
findings.detail: 'Every Stage-2 actor appears in at least one diagram' is set membership. ~200-400 tokens/run. Folds into the Mermaid validator.

opportunities.name: On Activation reproduces resolver-owned procedure as 25 lines of boilerplate
opportunities.description: Steps 1-5 spell out merge precedence, `file:` semantics, config-fallback order, and greeting/append execution — the standard 5-step on-activation procedure every BMad skill performs identically. Sibling skills inline only the resolver line plus a one-line greet/append, leaving the rest to the resolver script and the model. The hand-merge fallback paragraph encodes a procedure the LLM will not execute reliably, and the language-rules block at Step 5 is workflow-wide policy mixed into the greeting subsection.
opportunities.impact: Reclaims ~25 lines (~400 tokens) from the prompt, removes a fragile hand-merge fallback that wouldn't work if the resolver were truly absent, and lets the activation section read in one breath.
opportunities.action: Replace Steps 1-5 with the canonical activation block (resolve workflow -> run prepend -> load persistent facts -> load config -> greet and run append, one line each). Drop the manual TOML hand-merge fallback paragraph — if the resolver is missing, halt and report. Lift the 'Language rules for the entire workflow' subsection out of Step 5 into a separate paragraph above `## Workflow`.

findings.title: Steps 1-5 reproduce standard 5-step boilerplate
findings.detail: Lines 23-25 re-teach resolver merge rules; lines 31-33 re-teach `file:` persistent-fact semantics; line 37 enumerates five config files in order. All resolver-owned.

findings.title: Manual TOML hand-merge fallback is a fragile middle path
findings.detail: Tells the LLM to read three TOML files in base-team-user order and apply merge rules if the resolver fails. The LLM will not execute structural TOML merge reliably; either trust the resolver or halt-and-report on its absence.

findings.title: Step 5 mixes greet-and-execute with workflow-wide language rules
findings.detail: The 'Language rules for the entire workflow' subsection is workflow-wide policy, not part of greeting/append execution. Mixing them makes both subsections multi-purpose.

opportunities.name: Stage 1 is form-shaped where it should be facilitative — open-floor, intent-gate, and quick-confirm all missing
opportunities.description: Stage 1 dives into scanning `{planning_artifacts}` and fires a four-question grid (sources / mode / scope / diagram-type) with no open-floor invitation, no intent-confirm, no single inferred-defaults confirmation block, and no off-ramp for accidental activations. First-timers can't volunteer 'I have a Miro export here', experts have no quick-confirm path, confused users waste a full scan, and Stage 2's structured elicitation is single-lens (no capture-don't-interrupt slot, no parallel skeptic + actor-coverage lenses in Stage 4).
opportunities.impact: Cheapest delight win in the report — first-timers get to volunteer unexpected inputs, experts get a single-confirmation pass, confused users get an early off-ramp, and validation gains a parallel critical lens that catches the failure paths the authoring agent forgot to draw.
opportunities.action: Open Stage 1 with two sentences: an open-floor invitation ('share anything you already have — PRD path, sketches, prior flows, or just describe') and a one-line intent check ('capturing an existing flow, designing a new one, or both?'). Replace the four serial questions with one inferred-defaults confirmation block ('Inferred: migration mode, multi-flow, sequenceDiagram, four PRD docs — confirm or edit?'). Add a one-sentence off-ramp for clearly-not-a-business-flow inputs (class hierarchy, system architecture). In Stage 4, add a brief parallel skeptic + actor-coverage scout sub-step before finalizing.

findings.title: No open-floor opening or capture-don't-interrupt slot
findings.detail: Stage 1 starts with the scan; Stage 2 is structured-elicitation only. User cannot volunteer a Miro export, a hand-drawn photo, or a side-channel 'oh and there's an inventory-sync flow too'.

findings.title: No early intent gate before artifact scan
findings.detail: Skill scans `{planning_artifacts}` before confirming intent. Accidental activations and update-intent users both pay the scan cost.

findings.title: No quick-confirm path for experts (Yolo middle missing)
findings.detail: Four-question dance fires every run even when PRD makes the answers obvious. Three-mode architecture has only Guided + Headless.

findings.title: Validation is single-lens
findings.detail: Stage 4 is a five-item checklist run by the same agent that authored the diagram. Flow diagrams benefit sharply from a parallel skeptic + actor-coverage scout.

findings.title: No off-ramp for wrong-diagram invocations
findings.detail: Loose Vietnamese trigger 'tạo sơ đồ' (just 'create a diagram') can match class-diagram or architecture-diagram intent. No signpost to sibling skill.

opportunities.name: Workflow-type / structure surface mismatches and missing scaffolding
opportunities.description: The decision log claims Simple Workflow and the file is genuinely simple (~113 lines, all inline), but the `### Stage N` heading style matches Complex-Workflow shape — which is why the prepass infers `workflow_type: complex`. There is also no `## Overview` heading (the lead paragraphs serve as one but no mechanical scan sees it), Stage 1 packs workspace-binding mechanics and decision-log seeding into a single dense paragraph, and the `## On Complete` resolver call is a redundant second invocation when the activation already resolved the workflow block.
opportunities.impact: Aligns the file's claimed shape with its actual shape so scanners and authors read it consistently; makes Stage 1 single-purpose per paragraph; removes a wasted resolver call.
opportunities.action: Insert `## Overview` above the existing lead paragraphs (no content change). Rename `### Stage N: …` to neutral sectional headings (Prerequisites and Scope / Discovery / Diagram Generation / Validation / Save and Handoff) to honor the Simple-Workflow claim — or alternately accept Complex and update the decision log. Split Stage 1's workspace-binding paragraph into one bullet for workspace binding and one for decision-log seeding. Either note in Step 1 that the resolver returns the full workflow block including `on_complete` and have On Complete read it from memory, or document that re-resolution is deliberate.

findings.title: `## Overview` heading absent
findings.detail: Lead paragraphs carry role, mission, and BMM-phase position but sit under no heading. Prepass and mechanical scans miss it.

findings.title: Workflow-type claim vs heading style mismatch
findings.detail: Decision log says Simple; `### Stage N` headings are the Complex shape; prepass infers `workflow_type: complex`. The skill is genuinely Simple — the heading style is the only thing fighting the claim.

findings.title: Stage 1 packs workspace binding + decision-log seeding into one dense paragraph
findings.detail: Single sentence carries the workspace contract — workspace-binding mechanics and decision-log seeding should be separate bullets so the model doesn't disentangle them in one read.

findings.title: On Complete re-resolves the workflow block (wasted call)
findings.detail: Activation Step 1 already resolved `workflow.*` including `on_complete`. The second resolver call is harmless but wasted; either read from memory or document why it re-resolves.

opportunities.name: [ADDENDUM B1] Replace silent no-PRD fallback with HALT-menu (3 options)
opportunities.description: `SKILL.md:58` currently says 'switch to interactive elicitation' when no PRD found. BMad-native pattern is an explicit HALT menu with three options + `[q]` quit, not a silent fallback. This also closes the 'no-PRD + headless self-contradicting elicitation' branch in Opp #2.
opportunities.impact: Removes the silent-fallback ambiguity in interactive mode and the self-contradicting elicitation in headless mode. Makes pipeline behavior explicit and pageable.
opportunities.action: Replace `SKILL.md:58` with a HALT menu: '🚫 No PRD found in {planning_artifacts}. Options: [1] Run bmad-create-prd first, [2] Provide PRD path, [3] Continue interactive (greenfield, no source), [q] Quit'. In headless: only accept option 2 via `--prd-path=`, else return `blocked` with `reason: "no_prd_and_no_interactive_in_headless"`. Precedent: `bmad-create-story/SKILL.md:102-127`, `bmad-create-architecture/steps/step-01-init.md:89`.

findings.title: Silent fallback to elicitation when no PRD found
findings.detail: Current rule rolls into elicitation without menu — pipeline can't audit which branch fired.

opportunities.name: [ADDENDUM B2] Add `[A][P][C]` parallel-validation menu after Stage 3 and Stage 4
opportunities.description: BMad pattern for a parallel-validation lens: `[A] Advanced Elicitation / [P] Party Mode / [C] Continue`. `[A]` invokes bmad-advanced-elicitation to stress-test actor coverage; `[P]` invokes bmad-party-mode for multi-perspective review. HBC sibling INVEST already adopts this.
opportunities.impact: Catches the actor-coverage and flow-completeness gaps single-lens validation misses, without forcing every run through a heavier multi-agent pass. In headless: skip menu, default `[C]`, log auto-decision to .decision-log.md.
opportunities.action: Insert an `[A][P][C]` menu at the end of Stage 3 (after diagram generation, before Stage 4 validation) and at the end of Stage 4 (after validation, before save). Precedent: `bmad-create-architecture/steps/step-07-validation.md:26-37, 308-322`; `hbc-create-invest-epics-and-stories/references/story-generation.md:121`.

findings.title: No parallel-elicitation hook after Stage 3 (Diagram Generation)
findings.detail: Diagrams render and immediately enter single-lens validation. No moment to stress-test actor coverage before validation locks in.

findings.title: No parallel-elicitation hook after Stage 4 (Validation)
findings.detail: Validation findings are presented but no offer to invoke multi-perspective review before save. HBC sibling INVEST already has this hook.

opportunities.name: [ADDENDUM B3] Add template-existence check at Stage 1
opportunities.description: `{workflow.business_flow_template}` points to a path outside the default BMad install (`{project-root}/templates/D-06_business-flow-diagram_template.md`). HBC siblings don't validate template existence; this skill should set the precedent.
opportunities.impact: Replaces an opaque mid-workflow failure ('template not found' partway through rendering) with an early, actionable halt with remediation hint.
opportunities.action: At Stage 1 entry (before binding `{doc_workspace}`), check if `{workflow.business_flow_template}` exists. If not: halt with hint to run `hbc-setup` or clone the shared D-* template set. In headless: return `blocked` with `reason: "template_missing"`.

findings.title: Template-existence check absent
findings.detail: Skill proceeds into Stage 1 scan without verifying the configured template file exists. Failure mode is partway-through-render rather than upfront halt.

opportunities.name: [ADDENDUM B5] Establish `assets/decision-log-template.md` as HBC first-mover convention
opportunities.description: Neither HBC sibling (INVEST, setup) uses `.decision-log.md` — this skill is the first mover. Define a minimal schema now so future HBC skills follow consistently. Also addresses the structural issue in the original 'broken #2' (decision-log location).
opportunities.impact: Sets durable house style for HBC. Future HBC skills get a frontmatter convention (`stepsCompleted`, `inputDocuments`, `lastStep`) compatible with BMad-base resume protocols (see Opp #1 reinforcement).
opportunities.action: Create `src/hbc-create-business-flow-diagram/assets/decision-log-template.md` per HBC `assets/<*>-template.md` convention. Schema: frontmatter (`skill`, `phase`, `classification`, `last_touched`, `stepsCompleted`, `inputDocuments`) + per-session block (`## Session YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM — {Intent}` with Sources, Mode/Scope, Auto-fixes, Handoff target).

findings.title: No decision-log schema/template defined for HBC
findings.detail: HBC siblings don't have decision-log convention. This skill could become first-mover; defining a template now prevents drift across future HBC skills.

opportunities.name: [ADDENDUM B7] Backport BFD row to `hbc-setup/assets/module-help.csv`
opportunities.description: `hbc-setup/assets/module-help.csv` currently has one row (INVEST). The new BFD skill is registered in `_bmad/_config/bmad-help.csv:57` but is missing from the HBC module CSV. Without this, `hbc-setup` won't install the full HBC skill set in a new project.
opportunities.impact: Restores the install-time contract that `hbc-setup` covers all HBC skills. Without this row, fresh projects miss BFD entirely.
opportunities.action: Add a row to `hbc-setup/assets/module-help.csv` mirroring the `_bmad/_config/bmad-help.csv` BFD entry (skill name, display name, menu code, description, phase, before/after). Verify the `before`/`after` fields chain BFD correctly between PRD and Architecture in the planning phase.

findings.title: BFD missing from HBC module-help registry
findings.detail: `hbc-setup` install won't surface BFD as an installable HBC skill until this row is added.

opportunities.name: `output_dir` + `output_folder_name` split is a permutation surface
opportunities.description: Two scalars combine on activation to produce `{doc_workspace}`. The factoring lets a team choose parent dir and folder template independently, but in practice teams want one path. Users overriding `output_folder_name` to drop `{date}` won't realize `output_dir` is also configurable and will hit a half-override.
opportunities.impact: Halves the cognitive load of the output-path surface and removes the half-override risk. Also clears the path to fix the dated-folder-fights-resume issue cleanly.
opportunities.action: Collapse to one scalar `business_flow_output_path = "{planning_artifacts}/business-flows/D-06-{project_name}-{date}/"` and bind `{doc_workspace}` directly to the resolved value in Stage 1. Specify `{date}` format in the comment (commonly `YYYY-MM-DD`).

findings.title: Two-scalar output path forces a two-step mental model
findings.detail: `{doc_workspace} = {output_dir}/{output_folder_name}/` — independent factoring buys nothing in practice and risks half-overrides.

findings.title: `{date}` format unspecified in `output_folder_name` default
findings.detail: `D-06-{project_name}-{date}` — collide-or-accumulate on same-day re-runs depending on format choice (`2026-05-14` vs `20260514` vs `2026-05-14T19:23`). Specify in the comment.

strengths.title: Right size, right shape — inline-first Simple Workflow
strengths.detail: ~113 lines, all inline in SKILL.md + customize.toml, no `references/` carve-out, no scripts, no `assets/`. Matches the principles file's 'inline first, carve out only when needed' guidance for a single-artifact workflow.

strengths.title: Customization surface is clean and resisted the loudest temptations
strengths.detail: Six scalars + one persistent-fact array + two activation arrays. No boolean toggles, no hooks beyond `on_complete`, identity/persona kept out of `[workflow]`. The two anti-findings (migration-vs-greenfield as toggle, per-skill language override) were correctly *not* added — both are runtime per-conversation state.

strengths.title: Language-rule handling is explicit and correct
strengths.detail: Distinguishes `{communication_language}` (user-facing) from `{document_output_language}` (artifact-facing) and explicitly carves out Mermaid keywords as English. Exactly the kind of judgment context that earns its place in the prompt.

strengths.title: Downstream consumers named explicitly
strengths.detail: `bmad-create-architecture`, `bmad-create-ux-design`, `bmad-create-epics-and-stories`, `hbc-create-invest-epics-and-stories` — the workflow knows where it sits in the BMM phase chain, which makes the Stage 5 distillate handoff legible.

strengths.title: Graceful degradation on bmad-distillator
strengths.detail: 'Skip with a note if distillator is unavailable; never inline a substitute' — follows the principles file's graceful-degradation rule cleanly. (Template-absent and resolver-absent on On Complete still need the same treatment.)

strengths.title: Description format is well-disciplined
strengths.detail: Quoted trigger phrases including a Vietnamese trigger matching the configured `document_output_language`. Summary plus triggers, no padding.

strengths.title: Headless considered as a first-class mode at the structural level
strengths.detail: JSON return shape, decision-log auto-fix logging in headless, and 'absence of PRD is a normal mode' are all named. The contract isn't complete (see opportunities) but the foundation is there.

strengths.title: No deterministic work currently hidden in scripts
strengths.detail: Zero scripts means zero risk of 'intelligence leak into scripts' from regex-based meaning extraction. A clean baseline for the script-extraction work in the determinism findings — any added scripts should stay strictly in the plumbing lane.

architecture.assessment: A coherent, well-scoped Simple Workflow living entirely in `SKILL.md` + `customize.toml` — the right shape for its complexity. Routing, customization wiring, language discipline, and the Decision-Log Workspace seeding are all correct. Findings are tidy-ups: missing `## Overview`, a `### Stage N` heading style that contradicts the Simple claim, and ~25 lines of On-Activation boilerplate inherited from the build template.

determinism.assessment: The skill is mostly judgment work (actor identification, AS-IS/TO-BE delta, layout choices) and belongs in the prompt. But Stage 1's source-doc glob walk and Stage 4's three validation checks (Mermaid parse, actor coverage, FR traceability) are textbook script candidates — pure parsing and set-membership work the LLM currently re-does every run. A single `scripts/` directory with three small Python scripts converts this into a compact pre-pass/post-pass JSON exchange.

customization.assessment: Opted in with the full BMad always-present surface plus four workflow-specific scalars. All scalars, no arrays-of-tables, no boolean toggles, no hooks beyond `on_complete`. The two temptations from the context conversation (migration-mode toggle, per-skill language override) were correctly resisted. One real abuse remains — the `output_dir` + `output_folder_name` split should collapse to a single `business_flow_output_path`.

enhancement.assessment: Headless mode is named but not specified; the Decision-Log Workspace pattern is half-built (no resume, no Update, no compaction flush). Stage 1 trades open-floor warmth for a four-question form. The skill works on the first pass and falls apart on revisit — exactly the failure mode the Workspace pattern was designed to prevent.

journeys.summary: BA activating after `bmad-create-prd`. The four Stage-1 questions arrive as a flat batch with no anchor for consequences; greenfield-vs-migration is picked blind, losing the AS-IS the PM wanted. Bright spot: the workspace folder is created up front and the decision log captures the mode/scope choice, so a wrong pick is recoverable on a re-run.
friction_points: greenfield-vs-migration choice presented with no anchor for downstream impact
Four-question grid arrives with no open-floor invitation to volunteer unexpected inputs (Miro export, hand-drawn sketch, prior architect's notes)
bright_spots: Workspace + decision log capture mode/scope, making wrong picks recoverable on re-run

journeys.summary: BA on the fifth project this quarter — wants to slam through Stage 1 with 'use the PRD, migration, multi-flow, sequenceDiagram — go'. No Yolo / quick-confirm middle exists; the four-question dance fires every run even when the PRD's structure makes the answers obvious.
friction_points: No single inferred-defaults confirmation block — must answer four serial questions
No 'I've already structured the PRD with explicit AS-IS/TO-BE — just lift them' shortcut

journeys.summary: User says 'tạo sơ đồ' meaning a class diagram; trigger matches loosely enough that this skill activates. The skill scans `{planning_artifacts}` and asks about modes before the user realizes it's the wrong skill.
friction_points: No early intent-confirm before the artifact scan

journeys.summary: Genuine inputs the skill does not handle deterministically: zero-actor PRDs (pure data-pipeline products), sharded PRDs (fallback reads only index.md and misses substance), multiple PRDs in the same folder, no PRD AND no UX in headless mode, mis-marked migration on pure greenfield, and re-runs (no resume path; user gets fresh discovery on top of an existing artifact).
friction_points: Sharded PRD fallback reads only `index.md` — misses every actor and FR (high-severity, broken)
No resume protocol — re-run = fresh draft, prior reasoning lost
No targeted-update for a single flow — rebuilds everything
Multiple-PRD disambiguation absent — generous glob takes whatever it finds first
Zero-actor PRDs render a vacuous diagram (System talking to itself) — no guidance to promote trigger/cron to first-class actor
bright_spots: Falls back to interactive elicitation when no PRD present and explicitly calls this out as 'a normal mode, not a blocker'

journeys.summary: Resolver fallback exists for Stage 1 but not for the On-Complete resolver call. Template-file-missing has no fallback (skill halts). Compaction mid-Stage-2 loses the AS-IS/TO-BE discovery work because Stage 2 doesn't flush to the decision log. Bmad-distillator gracefully skipped (correctly).
friction_points: Template-file-missing halts the skill — no skeleton fallback
Stage 2 holds discovered state in conversation only — compaction drops it
On Complete resolver invocation has no fallback documented
bright_spots: `bmad-distillator` unavailable correctly skipped with a note, never inlined as substitute

journeys.summary: Pipeline calls `hbc-create-business-flow-diagram --headless` after `bmad-create-prd`. Stage 1's four interactive confirmations are not mapped to headless defaults, so different runs against the same PRD silently choose different modes/scopes/fixes. No documented input contract for overriding inferred defaults. When PRD is absent in headless, the skill contradicts itself by falling into elicitation instead of returning `status: blocked`. JSON return shape itself is correct and minimal.
friction_points: Headless defaults for each interactive decision unspecified — non-deterministic across runs
bright_spots: JSON return shape `{status, skill, primary, decision_log}` is minimal and correct (though `primary` is non-canonical)

autonomous.notes: Core artifact creation can run headless today, but Stage 1's four-question discovery and Stage 2's actor/trigger confirmation need an explicit headless-defaults table and an input contract for overriding them. The 'no PRD + headless' branch needs to return blocked rather than fall into elicitation.

recommendations.action: Wire the Decision-Log Workspace pattern fully: add resume-on-re-activation (Create/Update/Validate intents), flush Stage 2 discovered state to the decision log so compaction can't drop it, append (don't overwrite) revision-history rows on Update mode, and either document or fix the dated-folder-name fights-resume tension.

Specify the headless contract: a one-line headless default next to every interactive decision in Stages 1, 2, and 4; a documented input flag set (`--prd`, `--mode`, `--scope`, `--diagram-type`, `--no-prd-ok`); and a `status: blocked` branch when PRD is absent under `--headless`. Also rename/document the non-canonical `primary` JSON field.

Add a `scripts/` directory with three Python scripts — `discover-planning-artifacts.py`, `validate-mermaid.py` (folding actor-coverage in), `check-fr-coverage.py` — and wire them as pre-pass (S1 before Stage 2) and post-pass (S2-S4 in Stage 4). Recovers ~2400-5200 tokens/run and closes the headless-auto-fix quality risk.

Refactor Stage 1 into a facilitative shape: open-floor invitation + one-line intent gate + single inferred-defaults confirmation block (replacing the four-question grid) + one-sentence off-ramp for clearly-not-a-business-flow inputs. Add a parallel skeptic + actor-coverage scout sub-step before Stage 4 finalizes.

Collapse On Activation Steps 1-5 to the canonical 5-line block, drop the manual TOML hand-merge fallback (halt-and-report on resolver absence instead), and lift the workflow-wide language rules out of Step 5 into their own paragraph above `## Workflow`.

Tidy structural surface: insert `## Overview` heading, rename `### Stage N: …` to neutral sectional headings to honor the Simple-Workflow claim, split Stage 1's workspace paragraph into separate bullets for binding vs decision-log seeding, and have On Complete read `{workflow.on_complete}` from the already-resolved block instead of re-resolving.
Collapse `output_dir` + `output_folder_name` to a single `business_flow_output_path` scalar with `{date}` format specified in the comment.
Fix the broken sharded-PRD fallback rule (read every linked shard, not just `index.md`) and move `.decision-log.md` to `references/.decision-log.md` (or delete if it was build-time scratch).
[ADDENDUM] Add HALT-menu when no PRD found (B1) — replaces silent fallback in interactive mode; returns `blocked` with reason in headless. Resolves the no-PRD-elicitation contradiction in headless.
[ADDENDUM] Insert `[A][P][C]` parallel-validation menu after Stage 3 and Stage 4 (B2). Adopt the BMad multi-lens pattern already used by HBC sibling INVEST.
[ADDENDUM] Add template-existence check at Stage 1 (B3). Halt with remediation hint if `{workflow.business_flow_template}` missing; return blocked in headless.
[ADDENDUM] Create `assets/decision-log-template.md` as HBC first-mover schema (B5). Pairs with the Decision-Log Workspace work in Rec #1 and gives future HBC skills a consistent format.
[ADDENDUM] Backport BFD row to `hbc-setup/assets/module-help.csv` (B7). Without this, fresh projects miss the BFD skill at install time.

applied_changes.change: Renamed to templates/D-06_business-flow-diagram_template.md (Rule 1: file names always English). File was untracked; used `mv` not `git mv`.

Content rewritten as English i18n source. All Japanese headings/labels translated to English. AS-IS / TO-BE kept untranslated as business jargon (carve-out). Mermaid keywords and code identifiers kept original.
Updated `business_flow_template` path to point to the renamed template file.

Language-rules block rewritten with three-rule split: communicate in {communication_language}; template is English i18n source — translate to {document_output_language} at render time; file/folder names always English; carve-outs (Mermaid keywords/identifiers, AS-IS/TO-BE) stay original in both template and output.
RESOLVED broken #2. Moved src/hbc-create-business-flow-diagram/.decision-log.md → references/.decision-log.md. Confirmed by re-running scan-path-standards.py (structure category: 0 findings).

RESOLVED Op #7. Collapsed output_dir + output_folder_name → business_flow_output_path single scalar with {date}=YYYY-MM-DD documented in comment + guidance for resume override. Also tightened diagram_type comment to clarify it is a hint, not a contract (per Customization scanner anti-finding).
RESOLVED B7. Added BFD row mirroring _bmad/_config/bmad-help.csv:57 (phase 2-planning, after bmad-create-prd, before bmad-create-architecture).
RESOLVED B5. Created HBC first-mover decision-log schema (frontmatter + per-session block format) for runtime artifact at {doc_workspace}/.decision-log.md.

RESOLVED Op #3 (Path B) + B3. Created discover-planning-artifacts.py (includes template-existence check + sharded-PRD shard enumeration which RESOLVES broken #1), validate-mermaid.py (Mermaid syntax + actor coverage), check-fr-coverage.py (FR id covered/uncovered/phantom sets). All stdlib Python, smoke-tested OK.

RESOLVED Op #1 (Decision-Log Workspace pattern — Stage 1a resume detection with R/U/V/N/X menu, Stage 2 compaction flush to frontmatter + decision log, Stage 3 revision-history increment on Update mode), Op #2 (Headless Mode section with input flags table + defaults table + JSON contract — primary renamed to artifact, named reason values), Op #3 (scripts wired in Stage 1b and Stage 4), Op #4 (dropped hand-merge fallback, lifted Language Rules out of activation block — kept 5-step structure per anti-finding C), Op #5 (Stage 1 facelift: 1a-1e subsections with open-floor invitation, intent gate, no-PRD HALT-menu, inferred-defaults block, wrong-skill off-ramp), Op #6 (added ## Overview, renamed ### Stage N to neutral numbered subsections, On Complete reads from memory, split Stage 1 paragraph), B1 (HALT-menu Stage 1d), B2 ([A][P][C] menus at end of Stage 3 and Stage 4), B3 (template-existence check at Stage 1b reacting to discover script). Final size: ~200 lines (was ~113).
